etymology.text:
Etymology tree
Proto-Indo-European *kʷetwóres
Proto-Indo-Iranian *čatwā́ras
Sanskrit चतुर् (catúr)
Proto-Indo-European *h₂eng-
Sanskrit अङ्ग (áṅga)
Sanskrit चतुरङ्ग (caturaṅga)bor.
Middle Persian 𐭰𐭠𐭲𐭫𐭠𐭭𐭢 (cʾtlʾng /⁠čatrang⁠/)
Classical Persian چَتْرَنگ (čatrang)bor.
Arabic شطرنج
Borrowed from Classical Persian چَتْرَنگ (čatrang), from Middle Persian 𐭰𐭠𐭲𐭫𐭠𐭭𐭢 (cʾtlʾng /⁠čatrang⁠/, “chess”), borrowed from Sanskrit चतुरङ्ग (caturaṅga, “comprising elephants, chariots, cavalry, and infantry”), from चतुर् (catúr, “four”) + अङ्ग (áṅga, “limb, member”), literally “four limbs; four members”.

“ trouble departed, ” meaning that trouble departs from him who plays at it; (TA;) [or from
“ the royal care or sorrow; ” or from
“ six species or ranks, ” because the pieces are of so many species: (Richardson's Pers. Arab. and Engl. Dict., Johnson's ed.:)] or [accord. to some] it is from
; so says Ibn-Hishám El-Lakhmee: but, as IB and others have said, these derivations are only partial, making the
to be augmentative letters, and are manifestly incorrect. (MF, TA.)
